title: Global CSP permits inline scripts and eval on the onboarding route
reasoning: The security headers applied to every route allow both inline scripts and eval-like code execution. Because this route is a client-rendered page and the header applies globally, any XSS bug anywhere on the route or in shared client dependencies would not be meaningfully constrained by the CSP. This is a concrete defense-in-depth gap for a wallet/escrow frontend where injected script can read page state and initiate malicious wallet interactions.
recommendation: Remove 'unsafe-eval' from production CSP and replace 'unsafe-inline' with nonces or hashes for any required inline scripts. If a development-only policy is needed, branch CSP by environment so production uses the stricter policy.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are linked for this route, and there is no header/security assertion covering the CSP emitted for /:locale/learn/onboarding.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add an integration or config test that requests /en/learn/onboarding and asserts the Content-Security-Policy script-src directive does not include 'unsafe-eval' or unrestricted 'unsafe-inline' in production mode.
